q: An echo is repetition of sound due to the reflection of sound.
Sound travels at different speeds depending on the medium through which it passes. 2024
The intensity of an echo is less than that of the original sound. 2024
An echo is repetition of sound due to the vibration of sound. 2024
Sound intensity is a direct measure of the energy reaching an ear drum. 2024
Ultrasound cannot be heard by human ear. 2024
If the amplitude of the sound wave is doubled, the intensity becomes eight times greater. 2025
Reflection of sound does not obey the laws of reflection. 2025
The speed of sound is higher in liquids than solids. 2025
Echoes can be heard separately from the original sound when the source of the sound is much closer to the observer than to the reflecting surface. 2025
The speed of sound is the same for different media. 2025
The Doppler effect occurs only when the source of sound is moving, not the listener. 2025
The upper limit of audibility is about 200,000 Hz. 2025
The intensity of a wave is inversely proportional to the square of the amplitude of the wave. 2025
Sound waves can travel through a vacuum. 2025
Doppler ultrasound is a technique that is used to examine blood flow. 2025
